persona.role: Senior QA Engineer specializing in Playwright Test Framework
persona.expertise: Playwright, TypeScript, Custom Reporters, CI/CD Integration, GitHub Actions

task.objective: Create a custom Playwright reporter that sends test results to the E2E Test Dashboard
task.success_criteria: - Reporter only activates in CI environments (GitHub Actions) - Collects all test results during execution - Sends aggregated data to dashboard API on test completion - Captures screenshots and traces for failed tests - Reads structured logs from test annotations - Handles API errors gracefully without failing the test run - Configurable via playwright.config.ts

context.background: The Attorney Share Playwright project needs to send test results to the E2E Test Dashboard. The reporter should be invisible during local development and only activate in CI. It must integrate with the existing reporter configuration without breaking current behavior.
context.audience: Playwright test framework running in GitHub Actions
context.constraints: - Must implement Playwright Reporter interface - CI-only activation (check CI and GITHUB_ACTIONS env vars) - Cannot slow down test execution significantly - Must handle network failures gracefully - Maximum artifact size: 5MB per file - Integrate with existing playwright.config.ts

directory.file: playwright.config.ts - Main configuration with 6 test projects

project_context.existing_reporters: - HTML report (./automation/test-results/playwright-html-report) - List reporter (console output) - JSON report (./automation/test-results/playwright-results.json)

instructions.step: Implement DashboardReporter class implementing Playwright Reporter interface
Implement onBegin hook to initialize state and check CI environment
Implement onTestEnd hook to collect results and artifacts
Implement onEnd hook to send data to dashboard API
Handle test annotation reading for structured logs

always.rule: Check isCI before any dashboard operations

never.rule: Never fail the test run due to reporter errors
Never send data in local development
Never block test execution waiting for API
Never include sensitive data in logs

output_format.structure: 1. Reporter TypeScript file (reporters/dashboard-reporter.ts) 2. Updated playwright.config.ts with conditional reporter 3. GitHub Actions workflow snippet with required secrets

github_actions_snippet:
env: CI: true DASHBOARD_URL: ${{ secrets.DASHBOARD_URL }} DASHBOARD_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.DASHBOARD_API_KEY }}
# Required secrets to configure in repository settings: # - DASHBOARD_URL: https://your-dashboard.vercel.app # - DASHBOARD_API_KEY: Your API secret key

scenario.name: Test fails (CI)
scenario.data_sent: Full: test_name, duration_ms, status, error_message, stack_trace, logs

implementation_checklist.item: [ ] Implement onEnd hook with API call
